subject: Geography
chapter: The Origin and Evolution of the Earth

formulas.name: Law of Superposition
formulas.formula: Oldest layers are at the bottom and youngest layers are at the top.
formulas.notes: This law states that in any undisturbed sequence of rocks deposited in layers, the youngest layer is on top and the oldest on the bottom, each layer being younger than the one beneath it and older than the one above it.

formulas.name: Principle of Cross-Cutting Relationships
formulas.formula: Fossils and rock layers are cut by younger rocks and faults.
formulas.notes: This principle states that a fault or a rock cut by another rock is younger than the rock it cuts.

formulas.name: Principle of Inclusions
formulas.formula: Inclusions of one rock type within another indicate relative ages.
formulas.notes: This principle states that a rock inclusion within another rock is younger than the rock it is included in.

formulas.name: Principle of Fossil Succession
formulas.formula: Fossils in a specific order indicate relative ages.
formulas.notes: This principle states that certain fossils are found in a specific order in the geological record and that this order can be used to determine the relative ages of the rocks they are found in.

formulas.name: Continental Drift Theory
formulas.formula: Continents have moved over time, resulting in the formation of new oceans and mountain ranges.
formulas.notes: This theory states that the continents have moved over time, resulting in the formation of new oceans and mountain ranges.

formulas.name: Plate Tectonics Theory
formulas.formula: The Earth's lithosphere is divided into moving plates that interact at their boundaries.
formulas.notes: This theory states that the Earth's lithosphere is divided into moving plates that interact at their boundaries, resulting in geological phenomena such as earthquakes and volcanic activity.
